page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. The memory configuration consists of 2.0 GB of RAM, yielding 1.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPUs, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance is weak, ranking in the bottom 25% for stress-ng and bottom 10% for Geekbench multi-core. Memory bandwidth is limited, with read-only small block cached bandwidth scoring in the bottom 10% and large block read/write bandwidth in the bottom 25%. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average performance.

Given the 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and low memory capacity, this instance is constrained for memory-intensive applications. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, compute-bound tasks that benefit from dedicated AMD EPYC execution but do not require large memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include basic single-threaded processing, lightweight text processing, and simple web serving, while it is poorly suited for database operations, software compilation, or large-scale multi-threaded applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 vCPUs (1 physical core, 2 threads) and 2.0 GB of RAM. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a strong single-core CPU capability, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the lower percentiles. Single-threaded operations, such as gzip compression and text processing, perform at average levels. Due to its low 1.0 GB memory-per-core ratio, this instance is best suited for lightweight, compute-bound tasks, basic single-threaded processing, and simple web serving rather than memory-intensive database operations or complex multi-threaded workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 server with 2 vCPUs, 2 GB RAM, and a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU. It offers strong single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth and no local storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-2 features 2 vCPUs, 2 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, delivering strong single-core performance for light compute tasks.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU compute instance with strong single-core performance and a compact memory footprint for lightweight processing tasks.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical core
Compact 2.0 GB RAM configuration with 1.0 GB per core
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight, single-threaded compute workloads and basic web serving
